records.ety: [OE. proine, probably fr. F. provigner to lay down vine stocks for propagation; hence, probably, the meaning, to cut away superfluous shoots. See Provine.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Pruned (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Pruning.]

defs: To lop or cut off the superfluous parts, branches, or shoots of; to clear of useless material; to shape or smooth by trimming; to trim: as, to prune trees; to prune an essay.
quotes: Taking into consideration how they [laws] are to be pruned and reformed.
Our delightful task To prune these growing plants, and tend these flowers.

defs: To cut off or cut out, as useless parts.
quotes: Horace will our superfluous branches prune.

defs: To preen; to prepare; to dress.
quotes: His royal bird Prunes the immortal wing and cloys his beak.

defs: To dress; to prink; -used humorously or in contempt.

records.ety: [F. prune, from L. prunum a plum. See Plum.]

defs: A plum; esp., a dried plum, used in cookery; as, French or Turkish prunes; California prunes.
